text.title: API Documentation Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-documentation work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Docs lead with auth setup; first runnable example is 4 pages in; 60% of evaluators bounce.

root-cause.source: Stripe / Twilio docs guidance + Internal — abandonment audit 2024

antipattern.root-cause: Author treats docs as reference, not onboarding.
antipattern.fix: Put a runnable curl above the fold; auth token can be a test-only key. See rule quick-start-curl.

antipattern.symptom: Docs show response with field `created_at`; live API returns `createdAt`; client parses break.
antipattern.root-cause: Examples hand-typed and not regenerated when spec changed.
antipattern.fix: Render examples from openapi.examples block at build time. See rule examples-from-spec.

antipattern.symptom: Docs say "400 Bad Request" for every 4xx without distinguishing causes; partners hard-code on title strings.
antipattern.fix: Issue stable type URIs per error; link from Error Codes table. See rule error-table-linked.

antipattern.symptom: Breaking change ships; partner discovers in production at 3am.
antipattern.fix: One Changelog entry per spec_version bump with breaking flag + migration link. See rule changelog-per-version.
